title: taxodist
text:
Overview
taxodist provides a unified computational framework in R and Python for calculating phylogenetic distances between any two taxa. It leverages the deeply resolved hierarchical lineage data from The Taxonomicon, a comprehensive curated classification of all life based on Systema Naturae 2000.
Given two taxon names, the package retrieves their full lineages, identifies the most recent common ancestor (MRCA), and computes a dissimilarity index based on lineage depth:
\[
d(A, B) = \frac{1}{\text{depth}(\text{MRCA}(A,B))}
\]
The deeper the shared ancestor, the smaller (closer to zero) the distance. This tree-metric ensures that taxa diverging at the same node are always equidistant from any third taxon, regardless of downstream lineage depth differences.

title: taxodist
text:
Key Features

Phylogenetic Computation: Pairwise taxonomic distances, MRCA identification, and precise taxonomic path tracing.
Multivariate Ecology: Native generation of symmetric distance matrices ready for hierarchical clustering (taxo_cluster), Principal Coordinate Analysis (taxo_ordinate), and direct integration with vegan (R) or scipy (Python).
Robust Data Retrieval: Built-in session-level caching to minimize network overhead, automatic homonym disambiguation via strict numeric IDs, and graceful fallback handling for upstream server timeouts.

title: taxodist
text:
Quick Start
The API is strictly equivalent across supported languages, ensuring full reproducibility in multi-language bioinformatics workflows.

RPython


# Install from CRAN
install.packages("taxodist")

library(taxodist)

# Compute pairwise distance
taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor")

# Generate a distance matrix
theropods <- c("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor", "Spinosaurus", "Carnotaurus")
mat <- distance_matrix(theropods)


# Install from PyPI
pip install taxodist

import taxodist as td

# Compute pairwise distance
td.taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor")

# Generate a distance matrix
theropods = ["Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor", "Spinosaurus", "Carnotaurus"]
mat = td.distance_matrix(theropods)

title: taxodist
text:
An R and Python package for computing phylogenetic distances between any two taxa.
    Given two taxon names, taxodist retrieves their full hierarchical lineages from
    The Taxonomicon and computes a dissimilarity index based on the depth of their
    most recent common ancestor.
  
  
    
      
    
    
      
    
    
  
  
    Get started
    Reference
    GitHub
Overview

title: taxodist
text:
Installation and basic usage

RPython


install.packages("taxodist")
library(taxodist)

# Pairwise distance
taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor")
#> Distance : 0.01538   MRCA: Tyrannoraptora (depth 65)

taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Homo")
#> Distance : 0.02778   MRCA: Amniota (depth 36)

taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Quercus")
#> Distance : 0.25000   MRCA: discaria (depth 4)

# Distance matrix and clustering
taxa <- c("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor", "Triceratops",
          "Homo sapiens", "Panthera leo", "Quercus robur")
mat <- distance_matrix(taxa)
cl  <- taxo_cluster(taxa)
plot(cl)


pip install taxodist
import taxodist as td

# Pairwise distance
td.taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor")
# distance: 0.01538   mrca: Tyrannoraptora (depth 65)

td.taxo_distance("Tyrannosaurus", "Homo")
# distance: 0.02778   mrca: Amniota (depth 36)

# Distance matrix and clustering
taxa = ["Tyrannosaurus", "Velociraptor", "Triceratops",
        "Homo sapiens", "Panthera leo", "Quercus robur"]
mat = td.distance_matrix(taxa)
cl  = td.taxo_cluster(taxa)
cl.plot()



Functions
API reference


  
    Distance computation
    
      taxo_distance()Pairwise distance between two taxa
      distance_matrix()Full symmetric distance matrix for a set of taxa
      focal_distances()Distances from one focal taxon to a community
      closest_relative()Nearest taxon among a set of candidates
      mrca()Most recent common ancestor of two taxa
    
  
  
    Lineage and exploration
    
      get_lineage()Full lineage by taxon name
      get_lineage_by_id()Full lineage by Taxonomicon numeric ID
      get_taxonomicon_id()Resolve taxon name to numeric ID
      taxo_search()Search Taxonomicon; handles homonyms
      compare_lineages()Side-by-side lineage comparison at the MRCA
      shared_clades()Shared lineage trunk between two taxa
      taxo_path()Node-by-node path from taxon A to taxon B
      lineage_depth()Number of nodes in a taxon's lineage
      is_member()Test whether a taxon belongs to a clade
      filter_clade()Retain only taxa belonging to a given clade
      check_coverage()Verify which taxa are present in Taxonomicon
    
  
  
    Multivariate analysis
    
      taxo_cluster()Hierarchical clustering (UPGMA)
      taxo_ordinate()Principal coordinate analysis (PCoA)
      taxo_heatmap()Distance heatmap with marginal dendrograms
    
  
  
    Cache management
    
      clear_cache()Remove all entries from the session cache
      save_cache()Serialise the cache to an .rds file
      load_cache()Restore a previously saved cache from disk
      cache_info()Report cache contents and memory use
    
  



  Full reference



  Data source and citation
  
    All lineage data is sourced from The Taxonomicon,
    based on Systema Naturae 2000 (Brands, S.J., 1989 onwards).
    Please cite this resource in any published work that uses taxodist.
  
  
    Brands, S.J. (1989 onwards). Systema Naturae 2000. Amsterdam, The Netherlands.
    Retrieved from The Taxonomicon, http://taxonomicon.taxonomy.nl.
